one_liner: A 68-year-old retired construction foreman presents with 14 months of progressive exertional dyspnea and a dry, non-productive cough that has not responded to inhalers or a course of antibiotics.

stage2.hpi_extension: He initially noticed getting winded walking uphill but now becomes dyspneic walking across a room. His cough is dry and has not improved with guaifenesin or a recent Z-pack. He denies fevers, weight loss, night sweats, hemoptysis, or joint pains. He has no rashes, no Raynaud, and no dysphagia. He quit smoking 10 years ago (30 pack-year history). He denies asbestos exposure, bird keeping, or mold exposure.
stage2.social_history: Former smoker (30 pack-years, quit 10 years ago), retired construction worker, no known asbestos exposure, no pets, no birds, no farming

vitals.spo2: 88% on room air (drops to 82% with ambulation)

stage2.physical_exam: General: thin elderly man, comfortable at rest. Pulmonary: fine bibasilar end-inspiratory 'Velcro-like' crackles that do not clear with cough. No wheezes. Cardiac: regular, no murmurs, loud P2. Extremities: digital clubbing of fingers and toes, no cyanosis at rest. Skin: no rash, no sclerodactyly, no calcinosis. No synovitis.

lab_results.result: High-resolution chest CT: bilateral subpleural and basal predominant reticular opacities with honeycombing and traction bronchiectasis. No ground-glass opacities, no nodules, no mediastinal lymphadenopathy. Pattern consistent with usual interstitial pneumonia (UIP).
lab_results.clinical_significance: Subpleural, basal-predominant honeycombing with traction bronchiectasis defines the UIP pattern on HRCT — characteristic of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis. A UIP pattern in the absence of an identifiable cause is diagnostic of IPF without need for biopsy.

lab_results.result: Pulmonary function tests: restrictive pattern — FVC 58% predicted, TLC 62% predicted, FEV1/FVC 0.84 (preserved/elevated ratio). DLCO markedly reduced to 42% predicted. 6-minute walk distance reduced with desaturation to 82%.
lab_results.clinical_significance: Restrictive physiology (reduced FVC, TLC, normal or elevated FEV1/FVC ratio) with a markedly decreased DLCO is the classic PFT pattern of interstitial lung disease/fibrosis.

lab_results.result: ANA, rheumatoid factor, anti-CCP, anti-Scl-70, anti-Jo-1, anti-Ro/La, ANCA — all negative. Hypersensitivity pneumonitis panel (avian, Aspergillus precipitins) negative.
lab_results.clinical_significance: Negative autoimmune and hypersensitivity serologies exclude connective tissue-associated ILD (scleroderma, RA, dermatomyositis) and hypersensitivity pneumonitis, supporting the diagnosis of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis.

stage5.final_clue: He is started on pirfenidone (an antifibrotic), referred for supplemental oxygen and pulmonary rehabilitation, and placed on the lung transplant evaluation list given his rapidly progressive course.

debrief.classic_presentation: Idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis typically presents in men over 60 years, often former smokers, with insidious progressive exertional dyspnea and dry cough lasting months to years. Examination reveals fine bibasilar 'Velcro' crackles and digital clubbing. PFTs show a restrictive pattern with decreased DLCO. HRCT shows subpleural/basal honeycombing (UIP pattern). Median survival from diagnosis is 3–5 years.
debrief.pathophysiology: IPF is characterized by repetitive epithelial microinjury triggering abnormal wound healing: fibroblast proliferation, myofibroblast foci, excess extracellular matrix deposition, and architectural distortion with honeycomb cysts. The histologic pattern is usual interstitial pneumonia (UIP) — patchy, temporally heterogeneous fibrosis with subpleural/paraseptal distribution, fibroblast foci at the advancing edge, and honeycomb change. Unknown triggers; risk factors include smoking, genetic predisposition (telomerase mutations — TERT, TERC), and GERD (microaspiration).
debrief.treatment_overview: No therapy reverses fibrosis. Antifibrotic agents — pirfenidone (TGF-β and fibroblast inhibitor) and nintedanib (tyrosine kinase inhibitor targeting PDGFR, VEGFR, FGFR) — slow decline in FVC. Supportive care: supplemental O2, pulmonary rehabilitation, influenza/pneumococcal vaccination, treatment of GERD. Lung transplant is the only definitive therapy and should be considered early. Avoid corticosteroids (ineffective and harmful in IPF, though used in other ILDs).
high_yield_pearls: Idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis = UIP pattern on HRCT (bilateral subpleural/basal honeycombing + traction bronchiectasis) in the absence of known cause.
PFT pattern: restrictive (↓FVC, ↓TLC, normal or ↑FEV1/FVC ratio) with markedly decreased DLCO.
Physical exam hallmarks: fine bibasilar 'Velcro' crackles and digital clubbing.
Pirfenidone and nintedanib slow progression; no treatment reverses fibrosis. Lung transplant is the only definitive therapy.

Causes of restrictive lung disease: interstitial (IPF, sarcoidosis, pneumoconioses, drug-induced), extraparenchymal (obesity, kyphoscoliosis, neuromuscular disease), ARDS. Drug-induced: bleomycin, amiodarone, methotrexate, busulfan.
sources: First Aid for the USMLE Step 1 (2017) — Respiratory chapter, 'Restrictive lung diseases — Idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis' section.
